data.title: If elevated energy and food prices persist, they will fuel inflation worldwide

Fed's Powell: We have a resilient financial system now
Iran war is dimming outlook for many economies, IMF says
data.description:
WASHINGTON, March 30 (Reuters) - The war in the Middle East has caused serious disruption to the economies of frontline countries, and is dimming the outlook for many economies that had just started to recover from previous crises, the International Monetary Fund warned on Monday.

In a blog published by the global lender's top economists, the IMF said the war launched by U.S. and Israeli strikes against Iran on February 28 was causing a global, but asymmetric shock and leading to tighter financial conditions.

(Reporting by Andrea Shalal; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama)

By Andrea Shalal

data.title: Siemens plans reorganisation of two divisions, says source
data.description:
March 30 (Reuters) - Siemens is set to dissolve its digital industries and smart infrastructure segments in an upcoming reorganisation, a person familiar with the matter told Reuters on Monday.

The reorganisation seeks to break down "silo structures" in a move aligned with CEO Roland Busch's "One Tech Company" strategy, which aims to combine the likes of infrastructure and transportation with software and AI.

Digital industries currently focuses primarily on factory automation, motion control and process automation, while smart infrastructure's key business areas include building technology and energy management.

The two divisions would be split into six or seven smaller units in total, with a reorganisation of the executive board to follow, the source said. Each unit would report directly to the board.

How exactly this would look is still unclear, and the plan needs to be discussed by the supervisory board and employee representatives, likely in May.

Previous plans to merge the digital industries and smart infrastructure divisions have thereby been put on hold again, the source added.

The German engineering group's mobility division, which focuses on rail technology, is meanwhile expected to remain unaffected by the restructuring.

German newspaper Handelsblatt first reported on the plans.

Siemens declined to comment.

(Reporting by Alexander Huebner, writing by Marleen Kaesebier, editing by Sabine Wollrab and Miranda Murray)

Ferrari executes EUR24 million buyback in Milan and USD7 million on NYSE
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Ferrari NV announced on Monday that it repurchased 86,000 of its own common shares between March 23 and March 27 on the Italian stock exchange.

The shares were acquired at an average unit price of EUR279.5885, for a total consideration of nearly EUR24 million.

During the same period, the Prancing Horse company repurchased 21,859 common shares on the New York Stock Exchange. The US market transactions were executed at an average unit price of USD320.2166, totaling USD7.0 million, equivalent to approximately EUR6.1 million.

As of March 27, the company held 17.4 million treasury shares, net of shares assigned under the company's equity incentive plan, representing approximately 9.0% of the total issued common share capital.

Ferrari shares are currently trading up 3.5% at EUR287.90 per share.

By Antonio Di Giorgio, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

data.description: The Board of Directors of FUJIFILM Holdings Corporation has authorized a buyback plan on March 30, 2026.

data.title: Trump Says Iran Really Wants to Settle the War
data.description:
President Donald Trump spoke to Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern on the phone Monday morning, telling her that Iran wants to settle and that the war is going "unbelievably well." Earlier in a Truth Social post, Trump repeated threats to destroy Iran's energy assets if the Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened soon.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

Oil Analyst McNally Warns Market to Brace for Potential US Escalation
data.description:
Bob McNally, founder and president at Rapidan Energy Group, says oil markets should fade speculation on a near-term ceasefire in the war in Iran. He explains why there is not a need for the US military to occupy Kharg Island to choke off oil exports from Iran.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: The Boeing Company Provides Investor Update to Shareholders
data.description: On March 30, 2026, The Boeing Company announced that it has provide an investor update to shareholders, stating that it believes that it believes that based on its existing governance practices and policies, including the right to call special shareholder meeting, and stating that a shareholder proposal submitted by John Chevedden regarding a proposal on Shareholder Right to Act by Written Consent, is not in the best interest of the Company shareholders. In addition, the Company recommended the shareholders to vote against the proposal at the annual meeting of shareholders.
